signal_title: Child Safety and Well-Being Act of 2024
risk_rationale: The draft bill proposes the establishment of an independent Children's Commission to oversee child safety, which may influence future regulatory requirements but does not immediately conflict with existing TikTok policies.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Minimum registration age is 13 years globally, with regional variations.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: No specific age requirement mentioned, but oversight by a Children's Commission could lead to future changes.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: Potential future changes to age requirements or oversight mechanisms.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Screen time management with a 60-min default for users under 18, with a bypass code.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: No specific requirement mentioned, but oversight by a Children's Commission could lead to future changes.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: Potential future changes to screen time management policies.

remediations.title: Monitor Legislative Developments
remediations.description: Continuously monitor the progress of the Child Safety and Well-Being Act of 2024 and any subsequent regulations or guidelines issued by the proposed Children's Commission.
acceptance_criteria: Impact assessment of any new guidelines issued by the Children's Commission

business_impact: Currently low impact, but potential for future regulatory changes that could affect user registration and content management policies.
recommended_actions: Monitor the legislative process of the Child Safety and Well-Being Act of 2024.
Engage with policymakers to understand potential future requirements.
Prepare for potential adjustments to age-gated controls and screen time management policies.
